# Helmsman — Skills Catalog (generated)

<!-- GENERATED by skills/harness-audit/scripts/gen-catalog.mjs — do not edit by hand.
     Regenerate: node skills/harness-audit/scripts/gen-catalog.mjs -->

The single place to discover **which skill to use**. Each row: the skill, the agents that
own it (from their `skills:` frontmatter), and its "Use when" trigger (from the skill's
`description:`). To USE a skill, `read` its `SKILL.md` under `.helmsman/skills/<name>/`
(see operating-rules Rule 4). All skills are first-party (privatized).

## Helmsman skills (59)

| Skill | Owner agent(s) | Use when |
|-------|----------------|----------|
| `adherence-check` | @helm | Use when "are agents following the harness", "measure drift", "check adherence", or on a periodic harvest. |
| `autoplan` | @product | Use when the user has a plan and wants it fully reviewed without answering 15–30 intermediate questions — "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews". |
| `autoresearch` | @backend, @design, @eng, @mobile | Autonomous iterative optimization loop for maintenance metrics (test coverage up, bundle size down, lint errors to zero). |
| `brain-sync` | @helm | Use when the user says "sync the brain", "pull the brain", "start my session", "push the brain", "save the brain", or works across two machines on the same Supabase brain. |
| `browse` | @ship | Drive a headless/real browser to verify a deploy, dogfood a user flow, take annotated screenshots, check responsive layouts, test forms/uploads/dialogs, and ass |
| `calibrate` | @helm | Use when the user asks "how calibrated are we / were our bets right / are our estimates trustworthy", or on a periodic harvest. |
| `capture` | @helm | Write-back (Loop A), mode-aware. |
| `challenge` | @backend, @eng, @mobile | Pre-flight adversarial review before a high-risk build. |
| `claude` | @ship | Get an independent second opinion from another model/CLI on a risky diff — review, adversarial challenge, or a read-only repo consult. |
| `code-review` | @backend, @eng, @mobile, @ship | Production-readiness code review. |
| `context` | @helm | Save or restore working context across sessions/handoffs — capture git state, decisions, and remaining work (save), or load the most recent saved state to resum |
| `cso` | @backend | Chief Security Officer audit — infrastructure-first + OWASP Top 10 + STRIDE, with secrets, dependency supply chain, CI/CD, and LLM/AI security. |

| `devex-review` | @backend | Live developer-experience audit — actually TEST the DX: walk the docs, try getting-started, time TTHW, screenshot error messages, evaluate CLI help. |
| `diagnose` | @eng | Use when the user says "diagnose/debug this", reports something broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression. |

| `evolve-memory` | @helm | Use when an agent keeps re-learning the same lesson, "turn our learnings into a skill", "what patterns have we repeated", or periodically to harvest durable knowledge into skills. |
| `gbrain-upgrade` | @helm | Use when asked to "upgrade gbrain", "adapt to new gbrain", "check gbrain version", or after an upstream release. |
| `generate-plan` | @backend, @eng, @mobile | Write a blast-radius implementation spec to process/active/. |
| `grill` | @eng | Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan, "grill me", pressure-test a design, or (docs mode) challenge it against the project's domain model + docs. |
| `gstack-upgrade` | @helm | Upgrade the external gstack tool when it's installed and the project uses it — detect install, run its upgrade, surface what changed. |
| `handoff` | @chief | Compact the current conversation into a handoff document so a fresh agent/session can continue the work. |

| `qa` | @ship | Systematically QA a web app — two modes: report (find + document bugs, never fix) or fix (find, fix in source, re-verify each atomically). |
| `refine` | _(shared / ritual)_ | Self-refine (Loop B). |

| `scrape` | @chief, @growth, @ship | Pull structured data from a web page (read-only) — navigate, extract, return JSON. |
| `setup-deploy` | @backend | Configure deployment settings so land-and-deploy works automatically — detect platform, production URL, health-check endpoints, deploy + status commands; write |

| `ship` | @ship | Use when code is ready: "ship it", "deploy", "push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push". |
| `start` | _(shared / ritual)_ | Use when the user says "start", "/start", "begin", "what was I doing", or sits down to work. |

| `to-prd` | @eng, @growth, @product | Use when the user wants a PRD from the current context. |
| `triage` | @eng | Move issues through a small state machine of triage roles on the project issue tracker. |
| `understand` | @scout | Use when you need to learn a repo, ask "where is X / how does Y work / what depends on Z", explain a file, assess a diff's blast radius, extract domain flows, or onboard to a project. |

| `xia` | _(shared / ritual)_ | Use when you want to port a pattern or feature from an external repo. |
| `zoom-out` | @scout | Use when unfamiliar with an area of code, or when you need the higher-level picture of how something fits into the whole. |
